jokes.text: The only thing I am crazy about is a magnificent, pregnant manta ray named Ann Perkins.
jokes.setup: Leslie calls Chris crazy for something
jokes.punchline: Chris's overly elaborate and bizarre metaphor comparing Ann to a pregnant manta ray
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris Traeger character moment - his tendency toward bizarre, overly positive metaphors. The 'magnificent pregnant manta ray' is both absurd and oddly touching.

jokes.text: I can talk about Sandra Bullock skirt length.
jokes.setup: Chris offers to be a good sounding board for female friendship topics
jokes.punchline: His hilariously specific and random example of what he can discuss
jokes.explanation: Chris's earnest attempt to relate to female conversation topics with an oddly specific example. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: No, no, babe, you can't--you would just embarrass yourself. Plus, every time I start talking to you, five seconds later, I want to jump your bones.
jokes.setup: Chris offers to help with girl talk
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt assessment that he'd embarrass himself plus overshare about their sex life
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie overshare moment that derails the conversation in a believable way. The 'jump your bones' line is very Leslie.

jokes.text: I always knew there would be a day when I would get through to you and you would love government work. Today is that historic day.
jokes.setup: Ron volunteers for park survey work
jokes.punchline: Leslie completely misreading Ron's motivation as newfound love for government
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's eternal optimism and complete misunderstanding of Ron's true motivations.

jokes.text: Yes. That is what has happened.
jokes.setup: Leslie's enthusiastic misinterpretation of his motivations
jokes.punchline: Ron's perfectly flat, sarcastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson deadpan delivery. The economy is excellent - maximum comedy with minimal words. Classic Ron response.

jokes.text: Spending the day outside alone sounds like a dream.
jokes.setup: Context that he's supposed to be excited about government work
jokes.punchline: Ron revealing his real motivation - solitude
jokes.explanation: Reveals Ron's true motivation in character-appropriate way. The word 'dream' is perfectly Ron-like understatement.

jokes.text: I love being a father, but there are a few things I miss. Silence, the absence of noise, one single moment undisturbed by the sounds of a children's TV program called Doc McStuffins.
jokes.setup: Ron's talking head about being a father
jokes.punchline: His specific complaint about the omnipresent children's show
jokes.explanation: Relatable parent humor with Ron's characteristic specificity. The escalation from 'silence' to 'absence of noise' is good.

jokes.text: There is no quiet anymore. There is only Doc McStuffins.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration that Doc McStuffins has consumed his entire reality
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and economy. The cosmic finality of 'There is only Doc McStuffins' is both absurd and relatable to parents.

jokes.text: Shotgun on all rides for the rest of the day. For the rest of my life. In any car! Ha ha! I just faced you suckers!
jokes.punchline: His escalation to claiming shotgun rights forever and his made-up phrase 'faced you suckers'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation pattern typical of Andy. 'I just faced you suckers' is a nice Andy-ism that sounds like something he'd make up.

jokes.text: It's a business theory I'm working on.
jokes.setup: Tom asking about tent guy's handedness to determine hair part
jokes.punchline: His serious presentation of this absurd 'business theory'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom pseudo-business nonsense delivered with complete confidence. The absurdity fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: I don't have my straightforward deal fedora on me. We gotta stop by my storage unit on the way up.
jokes.punchline: Tom's revelation that he has specific fedoras for different types of business deals
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tom absurdism. The specificity of having different fedoras for different deal types is both ridiculous and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Remember you said that when Larry's farting up the car.
jokes.punchline: Tom's immediate practical concern about Larry's bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry joke setup. Not particularly original but serves the character dynamics.

jokes.text: And frankly, I don't care who knows that I said that, as long as no one knows that I said that.
jokes.setup: Ben stating his opinion about Larry being nice
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction of his brave stance
jokes.explanation: Good logical contradiction joke. The structure works well with the immediate reversal creating the comedy.

jokes.text: Ugh! God, why'd you let me say all that?
jokes.setup: Leslie oversharing about her elaborate Ann backup plans
jokes.punchline: Her immediate regret and blame-shifting to April
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Leslie's self-awareness after oversharing. The blame-shifting is in character.

jokes.text: Listen to me very carefully. I have not been taken. I know that's always your first fear when I'm not available, but this is not a Liam Neeson Taken scenario.
jokes.setup: Ann leaving a voicemail to address Leslie's concerns
jokes.punchline: Her knowledge that Leslie's first assumption is always kidnapping
jokes.explanation: Excellent character callback that shows how well Ann knows Leslie's paranoid tendencies. The Taken reference is specific and fitting.

jokes.text: That's exactly what they'd make you say.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's paranoid response that proves Ann's point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie paranoia escalation. The economy is excellent and it perfectly demonstrates her mindset.

jokes.text: What's good, baby? What's going down in Donna town? What's the haps in Meagle-wood?
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to connect with Donna
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt at cool slang and made-up place names
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Leslie trying too hard to be cool. 'Meagle-wood' is a nice touch that shows her effort.

jokes.text: I'm listening to Jaleel Or No Deal. It's a podcast where the kid who played Urkel reviews old Deal Or No Deal episodes. It's pointless, and I love it.
jokes.punchline: The incredibly specific and absurd podcast concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept that feels both completely made up and completely plausible. The specificity and Donna's honest assessment make it work.

jokes.text: What's the 411, little mama? What's the hot goss? Who you crushing on these days?
jokes.setup: Leslie continuing to try to connect
jokes.punchline: Even more awkward slang attempts including 'little mama' and 'hot goss'
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Leslie's awkward attempts. The cringe is earned through character behavior.

jokes.text: Ew, my husband, weirdo.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking who April is crushing on
jokes.punchline: April's disgusted reaction to the question and obvious answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - deadpan, economical, and shows her annoyance at Leslie's forced casual tone.

jokes.text: You sound like a tampon commercial.
jokes.punchline: April's blunt comparison to advertising
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that's very April. The comparison is specific and cuts through Leslie's enthusiasm effectively.

jokes.text: Wait, I thought you weren't gonna do Galentine's day this year because that she-beast moved away. I believe her name was Satan?
jokes.punchline: April's exaggerated evil characterization of Ann
jokes.explanation: Good callback to April's consistent dislike of Ann. The escalation from 'she-beast' to 'Satan' is nicely done.

jokes.text: Side note, do not Google that phrase.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing 'women's-only marathon bonding session with tons of whipped cream'
jokes.punchline: Her awareness of how that sounds when googled
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where Leslie shows awareness of innuendo. The warning is both funny and probably necessary.

jokes.text: You know, like, when you go to the ATM and you get money, is there an actual guy standing behind the wall who slides dollar bills in there?
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding of how ATMs work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy stupidity that's both absurd and oddly logical from his perspective. The visual image is funny.

jokes.text: It's robots.
jokes.setup: Ron saying no to the ATM guy theory
jokes.punchline: Andy's immediate pivot to an equally wrong but confident answer
jokes.explanation: Great economy and Andy's confidence in his wrong answer is perfect. The quick pivot shows his thought process.

jokes.text: In the movie Predator, can you rub any kind of mud on yourself to prevent predator from seeing you with his thermal heat signature sight, or is it more--
jokes.punchline: His overly detailed question about Predator movie logistics
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his focus on important movie details. The specificity is characteristic of his interests.

jokes.text: Oh, whoa. Five-second rule. No, ahh.
jokes.punchline: His attempt to apply five-second rule to his own mouth injury
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment where Andy applies food rules to injury. The logic is absurd but very Andy.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, Larry. Your tuna fish sandwich stunk up my suit.
jokes.punchline: Tom's dramatic complaint about smell contamination
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry abuse from Tom. Not particularly creative but serves character dynamics.

jokes.text: I got over 1,300 tents in this warehouse alone. 900 of those are over 2,000 square feet, 80 of 'em are striped, and exactly 4 of 'em were once rented by Miss Rene Russo.
jokes.setup: Harvey explaining he's serious about tents
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific statistics ending with random Rene Russo fact
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation building to the absurd Rene Russo detail. The specificity sells the character as an obsessive tent expert.

jokes.text: I was gonna go with my boyfriend, but he couldn't come because he decided to stay with his wife.
jokes.setup: Shauna explaining her solo Rome trip
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that she's dating a married man
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy delivered casually. The matter-of-fact delivery makes the uncomfortable truth funnier.

jokes.text: Where do you think I got that crystal Buddha head above my jacuzzi?
jokes.punchline: Donna's casual mention of her elaborate home setup
jokes.explanation: Good Donna moment showing her mysterious wealth and lifestyle. The specificity of 'crystal Buddha head above my jacuzzi' is perfect.

jokes.text: Shauna Malwae-Tweep. Pretty, fragile, makes terrible life decisions. A real fixer-upper, but look, it's not like Ann was doing so hot when I met her.
jokes.punchline: Her brutally honest assessment and comparison to Ann
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie analysis showing her management approach to friendship. The 'fixer-upper' comparison to Ann is both sweet and characteristic.

jokes.text: And then we got Evelyn, aka fake Ann. I don't know her last name. Honestly we just needed bodies.
jokes.punchline: Her brutal honesty about Evelyn's purpose
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Leslie's practical approach. 'We just needed bodies' is perfectly blunt.

jokes.text: Just for fun. Nothing serious. But please answer them with complete honesty 'cause I'll be able to tell if you're lying.
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting her 'just for fun' claim with serious warnings
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie contradiction showing her inability to keep things casual. The threat detection is very her.

jokes.text: Well, for live tweeting, it's Scandal. For binge watching, it's Scandal. But for fashion, it's actually Scandal. My answer is Scandal.
jokes.punchline: Donna's commitment to Scandal across all categories
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with Donna's single-minded devotion. The repetition works well for the character.

jokes.text: Ooh, I love watching Russian traffic accidents on YouTube while I play children's music at the wrong RPM.
jokes.punchline: April's disturbing multimedia entertainment combination
jokes.explanation: Excellent April weirdness that's both disturbing and perfectly in character. The combination of elements is genuinely creative and unsettling.

jokes.text: I like beauty pageants. Everyone is so happy and perfect.
jokes.punchline: Shauna's need for artificial happiness after her life revelations
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Shauna's escapism. The contrast with her messy life is the joke.

jokes.text: I like Diagnosis Murder. And skin flicks.
jokes.setup: Elderly Ethel answering about TV preferences
jokes.punchline: Her casual admission to watching pornography
jokes.explanation: Good subversive humor from unexpected source. The casual delivery and economy make it work well.

jokes.text: The correct answer for favorite TV show is Friday Night Lights.
jokes.punchline: Leslie revealing this was a test with a predetermined answer
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie control-freak moment. The revelation that there was a 'correct' answer is very in character.

jokes.text: It costs extra to have people inside the tents? And what's a flap tax?
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous charges including 'flap tax'
jokes.explanation: Good absurd business practice humor. 'Flap tax' is a nice invented bureaucratic term.

jokes.text: Well, you need flaps, bro. A tent without flaps is basically a parachute.
jokes.punchline: Harvey's pseudo-logical explanation
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic that sounds almost reasonable. The parachute comparison is specific and visual.

jokes.text: Shh. Keep your voice down, please, there's tents here.
jokes.setup: Tom raising voice about tent costs
jokes.punchline: Harvey treating tents like they can be disturbed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of tent obsession - treating inanimate objects as living beings.

jokes.text: Now, look, you can argue with me all you want, but for every line item that you two idiots disagree with, there's gonna be a $50 discussion surcharge.
jokes.punchline: Harvey charging them for the act of disagreeing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Harvey's predatory business practices. The meta nature of charging for disagreement is both absurd and plausible.

jokes.text: What's the price now? $80 more. I liked it better the other way. Does have an effect, though.
jokes.setup: Tom trying his hair-parting business theory
jokes.punchline: It backfiring but Tom still believing in the method
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tom's earlier theory. His continued belief despite negative results is very Tom.

jokes.text: Do you know what my social security number is? Or if I'm allergic to anything? I don't like corn. Should I put that down? Or will that be confusing? Because I do like candy corn.
jokes.punchline: His stream of consciousness confusion about basic information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy confusion cascade. The corn/candy corn distinction shows his thought process perfectly.

jokes.text: All I wanted was two hours where I got to forget that I was a parent.
jokes.setup: Ron stuck with Andy at the dentist
jokes.punchline: His honest admission about needing a parental break
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ron's earlier motivation. Relatable parent humor delivered in Ron's honest style.

jokes.text: Ron, look. I found all three differences. Those are two completely different pictures.
jokes.punchline: Ron's deadpan correction of Andy's 'achievement'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy/Ron dynamic. Andy's pride in solving an impossible puzzle and Ron's matter-of-fact correction.

jokes.text: I've gotten two annulments. One for pleasure, and one to cap off a long con I was running against Keith Sweat.
jokes.punchline: Donna's casual mention of marriage fraud against a celebrity
jokes.explanation: Excellent Donna mystery escalation. The Keith Sweat specificity and casual delivery of criminal activity is perfect. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I'm writing a memoir based on my blog. It's called Tweeping Up Appearances, and it's about my journey to find happiness by smiling through the pain.
jokes.punchline: The terrible pun title and depressing subject matter
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with weak pun that fits Shauna's trying-too-hard personality. The contrast between title and content works.

jokes.text: It's simply a way for me to figure out which one of you is the best.
jokes.punchline: Her honest but tactless explanation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where Leslie's honesty backfires. The bluntness is very Leslie but socially disastrous.

jokes.text: You're better than that, Knope.
jokes.punchline: Donna's disappointed but caring response
jokes.explanation: Good reaction showing Donna's genuine care despite her disappointment. The economy is good.

jokes.text: Gayle always has me do the windshield. She likes to stand behind me and watch. According to her, it's the best view.
jokes.punchline: TMI about Gayle's appreciation of Larry's physical form
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable humor showing Larry's oblivious oversharing about his marriage.

jokes.text: I'm just not used to people from the office asking me things. It's kind of scary. But in a good way, though.
jokes.setup: Ben showing interest in Larry's life
jokes.punchline: Larry's honest reaction to basic human kindness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's low expectations and genuine appreciation. Touches on the workplace bullying theme.

jokes.text: Yeah. Yeah, it was loud. Smells like a hippopotamus took a dump on a skunk.
jokes.setup: Larry covering for Ben by claiming he farted
jokes.punchline: Ben playing along with an elaborate description
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing Ben's growing friendship with Larry. The animal comparison is vivid and gross.

jokes.text: Well, my grandmother was missing that tooth, and she was the most beautiful woman on her oil rig.
jokes.setup: Andy considering keeping his broken tooth
jokes.punchline: His grandmother story with unexpected oil rig detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent Andy family backstory that's both sweet and absurd. The oil rig detail is perfectly unexpected.

jokes.text: Like a cool uncle, but old. Like a grandpa. But cool like a son.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to define his relationship with Ron
jokes.punchline: His increasingly confused family metaphors
jokes.explanation: Good Andy confusion spiral. The contradictory family relationships show his thought process breaking down.

jokes.text: That's right, dickweed. You wanna talk deal? Because my prices have gone up ever so slightly since I saw you last.
jokes.setup: Harvey appearing at the coffee shop
jokes.punchline: His immediate hostility and price manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Harvey's antagonism. 'Dickweed' feels appropriately hostile but not too harsh for the show.

jokes.text: Tent Town, Rent Ten Tents, the Tent Offensive, and Ace Tentura Tent Detective.
jokes.setup: Harvey claiming to own all tent stores
jokes.punchline: His increasingly elaborate tent-themed business names
jokes.explanation: Excellent series of tent puns escalating in creativity. 'Ace Tentura Tent Detective' is particularly clever.

jokes.text: You've reached the Tentagon. How can I help you, buttface?
jokes.punchline: Harvey owning that one too with Pentagon pun
jokes.explanation: Good callback continuation with another clever pun. 'Buttface' adds to Harvey's juvenile hostility.

jokes.text: Although, when I was in college I found out that my dad had a second family. Well, we were the second family, technically.
jokes.setup: Shauna and Leslie finding common ground as only children
jokes.punchline: Shauna's casual revelation about family dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy delivered casually. The correction about being the 'second' family adds to the dysfunction.

jokes.text: Although there is a tiny part of me that always thought it was just Chris talking into a mirror.
jokes.punchline: Her suspicion about the therapist's existence
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about Chris's over-the-top personality. The mirror image is both plausible and absurd.

jokes.text: I don't know, I read a book once that said a woman should never make the first move. / That doesn't apply to therapy. Okay. Good luck!
jokes.punchline: Her confusion about applying dating advice to mental health
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Shauna's confused priorities. Ann's quick correction and escape is perfect.

jokes.text: I mean, you're always beautiful, but right now you are the most beautiful, glowing sun goddess ever.
jokes.punchline: Her typical over-the-top metaphorical praise
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-complimenting. The 'sun goddess' escalation is very her but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Fine. Let me just wolf down this peanut brittle real quick. Why would you do that? Oh, right. 'Cause calories.
jokes.setup: Andy agreeing to go back to dentist
jokes.punchline: His decision to eat hard candy with a broken tooth and illogical reasoning
jokes.explanation: Good Andy logic failure. The 'calories' reasoning shows his backwards thinking perfectly.

jokes.text: It's called Sweaty Roger's Pants Tent. Oh, no, you can't rent tents there.
jokes.punchline: The misleading business name and Tom's realization
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with the business name. The innuendo works for the setup but resolution is quick.

jokes.text: Never thought I would say it, but I'm a genius for making you stay outside and do a menial task.
jokes.punchline: Tom taking credit for accidentally good management
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment taking undeserved credit. The irony of calling accidental success 'genius' fits his character.

jokes.text: Nobody can match your ethnic hybrid energy.
jokes.punchline: Her awkward way of describing what makes Ann special
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie awkward compliment. 'Ethnic hybrid energy' is cringey but well-intentioned in Leslie's way.

jokes.text: Yeah, 'cause Riggins is a criminal. He took the fall for his brother. Okay, he didn't watch out for his brother.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ann debating Friday Night Lights characters
jokes.punchline: Leslie's passionate anti-Riggins argument
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's strong opinions about fictional characters. Shows their friendship dynamic.

jokes.text: I almost bought a toe ring the other day. What? Ann, you're somebody's mother.
jokes.punchline: Her shock at Ann's potential toe ring purchase
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie judgment about appropriate maternal behavior. The horrified reaction to toe rings is very Leslie.

jokes.text: When it's all said and done, there were a few mazes I'd cheated on. Also, the inside of my mouth is black and hot.
jokes.setup: Andy reflecting on his dentist experience
jokes.punchline: His confession about cheating and disturbing mouth description
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier maze moment. The juxtaposition of cheating confession with medical concern is very Andy.

jokes.text: So do I strip down to my underwear, or all the way naked?
jokes.punchline: His confusion about dental procedure requirements
jokes.explanation: Good Andy confusion about medical procedures. The innocent delivery makes the absurdity funnier.

jokes.text: So do I strip down to my underwear, or all the way naked?
jokes.setup: Andy asking about dental procedure protocol
jokes.punchline: His assumption that dentistry requires undressing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy confusion about basic procedures. His serious tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I've won two online satellite Jamaican poker tournaments. I can tell that you're bluffing.
jokes.setup: Larry threatening Harvey with code violations
jokes.punchline: Harvey's absurdly specific and irrelevant credentials
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Harvey's misplaced confidence. The specificity of 'satellite Jamaican poker' is absurd.

jokes.text: I am. And I was bluffing about winning those tournaments.
jokes.punchline: His immediate admission to lying about everything
jokes.explanation: Good quick deflation of Harvey's bluster. The economy is excellent and the confession feels realistic.

jokes.text: God! Can you just be cool for one second?
jokes.punchline: Tom's frustrated reaction to Larry being nice
jokes.explanation: Standard Tom/Larry dynamic moment. Not particularly original but serves the characters.

jokes.text: Like a solid B-minus.
jokes.punchline: April's grudging academic assessment of Leslie
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - grudging affection expressed through academic grading. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: We love your crazy ass too, Knope. / I think you're fine. Like a solid B-minus.
jokes.punchline: Donna's warm response contrasted with April's backhanded numerical rating
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast. April's grading system for friendship is perfectly her.

jokes.text: Hi, Leslie. It's Ann. I love you, and everything's gonna be okay.
jokes.punchline: Ann's voice coming from a stuffed animal
jokes.explanation: Sweet gesture that becomes funny through the medium. The sentiment is genuine but the delivery method is absurd.

jokes.text: Mm, you touch me and I stab the bunny.
jokes.punchline: April's threat to destroy the gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect April dark humor and boundary-setting. The threat is both funny and believable coming from her.

jokes.text: I'm just an impartial bunny, but I think Ann sucks. Also-- I'm the zodiac killer.
jokes.setup: April playing with the Ann bear
jokes.punchline: Her escalation from Ann criticism to serial killer confession
jokes.explanation: Excellent April dark humor escalation. The 'impartial bunny' framing makes the confession even funnier. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Okay, well, that's funny, 'cause I'm terribly allergic to pistachios.
jokes.setup: Tom sarcastically saying Larry's inhaler is under pistachio shells
jokes.punchline: Larry's serious response revealing real danger
jokes.explanation: Good setup where Tom's sarcasm accidentally becomes dangerous. Larry's matter-of-fact delivery works well.

jokes.text: He is a good friend and a fine man with an inexplicably gorgeous wife-- I mean, to the point where it makes no sense at all.
jokes.punchline: His honest confusion about Larry's marriage success
jokes.explanation: Good callback to running gag about Gayle. Ben's genuine confusion about the mismatch is relatable and honest.

jokes.text: O, captain, my captain.
jokes.punchline: His dramatic Dead Poets Society reference
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference that fits Ben's dramatic tendencies but isn't particularly creative.

jokes.text: Got so startled I tooted.
jokes.punchline: Larry's involuntary bodily function from emotion
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry physical comedy. The word 'tooted' is appropriately childish but the joke is predictable.
